spdx_expression_simple.description: SPDX license expression (basic pattern validation). Full SPDX parser validation is recommended.

id.description: Opaque publisher-defined identifier. Consumers MUST treat raw id as opaque and MUST NOT use it directly as a filesystem path, shell token, or URL path segment. If a local-safe derivation is unsafe or collides, implementations MUST fall back to the spore hash.

key.description: Optional pre-filled author public key. If absent, the publishing implementation MUST populate it from the current signing domain identity during release. If present, it MUST match that identity.

synopsis.description: One-line summary — agent reads this alone and understands what the spore does

intent.description: Multi-paragraph description of this spore's functionality and purpose — what it does, how it works, why it exists. Each array item is a paragraph. Permanent, not cleared on release.

mutations.description: What changed relative to the spawned_from parent — describes the mutations applied to derive this spore from its ancestor.

spore_core_released.description: Published spore core — the full spore_core as it appears in a released spore.json. Adds domain, key, size_bytes, updated_at_epoch_ms as required fields with proper type definitions (these are forbidden in the draft spore.core.json).

id.description: Opaque publisher-defined identifier. Consumers MUST treat raw id as opaque and MUST NOT use it directly as a filesystem path, shell token, or URL path segment. If a local-safe derivation is unsafe or collides, implementations MUST fall back to the spore hash.

key.description: Author's Ed25519 public key. Publishing implementations populate this from the current signing domain identity during release.

synopsis.description: One-line summary — agent reads this alone and understands what the spore does

intent.description: Multi-paragraph description of this spore's functionality and purpose — what it does, how it works, why it exists. Each array item is a paragraph. Permanent, not cleared on release.

mutations.description: What changed relative to the spawned_from parent — describes the mutations applied to derive this spore from its ancestor.

size_bytes.description: Total uncompressed source size in bytes — sum of all blob content sizes from tree hash computation. Populated by release tooling, not present in spore.core.json draft.

updated_at_epoch_ms.description: Content update timestamp (milliseconds since Unix epoch). Recommended source: latest Git commit time for the source tree, with max file mtime as fallback.

relation.description: Relationship type. Predefined: spawned_from, absorbed_from, depends_on, follows, extends. Custom types allowed.

id.description: Optional opaque alias for this bond. Consumers MUST NOT use raw id directly as a filesystem path, shell token, or URL path segment. If a local-safe derivation is unsafe or collides, implementations MUST fall back to the bond target hash.

reason.description: Optional explanation of why this bond exists and what role it plays. Typically omitted for spawned_from (use mutations field instead).

with.description: Bond-specific parameters whose schema is defined by the bonded spore's convention. Opaque to the CMN protocol.

exclude_names.description: Directories or files to skip during tree hashing. SHOULD include .git and .cmn at minimum.

follow_rules.description: Paths to ignore-style rule files (e.g., .gitignore) honored during tree hashing.

filename.description: Deprecated. Archive URL is now resolved via endpoints archive url template + spore hash. Ignored by clients.

type.description: Extension distribution protocol identifier (e.g., s3, bittorrent, rsync)
